articles.headline: Remembering the Green Ramp Disaster
articles.blurb: Tragedy struck on March 23, 1994, at Pope Air Force Base, North Carolina, on an aircraft parking ramp known as ‘Green Ramp.’ A number of paratroopers were lost and several injured after flames and debris rained down on their training and assembly area after a mid-air collision between a fighter j...
articles.takeaway: The 1994 Green Ramp disaster killed 24 paratroopers when a fighter jet collided with a transport plane overhead, highlighting critical risks of concurrent air operations near ground assembly areas.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across all aviation systems—from flight planning to ground handling—offers near-term efficiency gains that can partially offset growth-driven emissions while longer-term technological solutions develop.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts
